skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Access 250+ cloud AI models through a simple CLI - no GPU required
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. This is legitimate CLI documentation for an AI services marketplace. Commands flagged as 'external_commands' are markdown documentation examples, not executed code. The pipe-to-shell installation pattern is standard industry practice with SHA-256 verification. Network access is to the legitimate inference.sh service endpoints. Environment variable usage is documented API configuration for CI/CD. No malicious patterns detected.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains CLI Command Examples
medium_findings.description: The documentation contains examples of CLI commands using backtick syntax. These are markdown documentation examples, not executed code. Evaluated as false positive - legitimate documentation pattern for a CLI tool skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are markdown documentation showing CLI command examples, not actual shell execution. The backtick syntax in markdown is standard documentation practice.

low_findings.title: Standard CLI Installation Pattern
low_findings.description: The 'curl | sh' installation method is flagged as pipe-to-shell. This is standard industry practice used by npm, docker, and many CLI tools. The installer performs SHA-256 checksum verification and optionally Sigstore signature verification. Evaluated as false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pipe-to-shell is documented standard installation method with security verification steps included in documentation.

low_findings.title: Service URLs Classified as Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: URLs like https://cli.inference.sh are flagged as hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate service endpoints for the inference.sh platform, not secrets. Evaluated as false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Service endpoints are intentionally hardcoded as they are the legitimate platform URLs, not sensitive data.

content.user_title: Access 250+ AI Models via Command Line
content.value_statement: Running AI models requires expensive GPU hardware and complex setup. This skill provides instant access to 250+ AI models through a simple CLI with no local installation needed.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream models via simple commands
Create videos with Veo 3.1, Seedance, Wan 2.5, and OmniHuman
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi, and any OpenRouter model
Perform web searches using Tavily and Exa with structured results
Post tweets, send DMs, and manage Twitter/X accounts
Upload local files for AI processing with automatic cloud upload
limitations: Requires internet connection to access cloud API endpoints
Usage costs apply based on inference.sh pricing for each model
Rate limits and quotas depend on account tier
Some models may have regional availability restrictions

use_cases.title: Generate AI Images Without GPU Hardware
use_cases.description: Create professional images using FLUX, Gemini, or Grok Imagine directly from the command line. No local GPU or complex setup required.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and designers who need quick AI image generation

use_cases.title: Call Claude or Other LLMs for Task Automation
use_cases.description: Access Claude, Gemini, or any OpenRouter model to process text, answer questions, or integrate AI into scripts and workflows.

use_cases.title: Search the Web with AI Understanding
use_cases.description: Use Tavily or Exa to search the web and get AI-understood results, perfect for research tasks and data gathering.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and content creators gathering information

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a sunset over mountains using the inference.sh CLI

prompt_templates.title: Create Video from Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the Veo video generation app with a prompt about a drone flying over a forest
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - using specific model with custom input

prompt_templates.title: Call Claude for Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenRouter Claude endpoint to analyze this code and explain what it does: [paste code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - integrating AI into workflow with user input

prompt_templates.title: Batch Process with Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent AI news using Tavily, extract key points, and save results to a file
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - chaining commands with file output

output_examples.input: belt app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut"}'
output_examples.output:
Task started: abc123
Status: processing
Image URL: https://results.inference.sh/abc123.png

Available Claude models:
- openrouter/claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620
- openrouter/claude-3-opus
- openrouter/claude-3-haiku

best_practices: Use 'belt app sample' to generate input files before running apps for the first time
Store API keys in INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD integration
Use --no-wait flag for long-running tasks and check status later with 'belt task get'
anti_patterns: Do not share your INFSH_API_KEY in public repositories or logs
Avoid running many concurrent tasks without checking rate limits first
Do not hardcode specific task IDs - query status dynamically instead

faq.question: What models are available through this skill?
faq.answer: Over 250 models including FLUX, Veo, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, and any OpenRouter model.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with the CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'belt login' to open browser authentication, or set INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for programmatic access.

faq.question: Can I use local files with the AI models?
faq.answer: Yes, provide local file paths and the CLI automatically uploads them to the cloud before processing.

faq.question: What happens if a task takes a long time?
faq.answer: Use --no-wait to start tasks without blocking, then check status with 'belt task get <task-id>'.

faq.question: How do I find the right model for my task?
faq.answer: Use 'belt app list --search <query>' or 'belt app list --category <category>' to discover available models.

faq.question: Is there a cost to use these models?
faq.answer: Yes, usage is billed through your inference.sh account. Check pricing at inference.sh for per-model costs.
